feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship leaves Cape Verde after three evacuated
entry.summary: A British man is among three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms while aboard the MV Hondius.

entry.title: RNA-triggered cell killing with CRISPR–Cas12a2
entry.summary: Cas12a2 enables RNA-triggered, sequence-specific killing of eukaryotic cells via widespread DNA shredding, allowing selective elimination of cells on the basis of gene expression, including virus-infected or mutation-bearing cells.

entry.title: 'This is a big outbreak': Nearly 1 in 5 affected by TB at SF school
entry.summary: New testing is starting at Archbishop Riordan High School as officials work to keep tuberculosis cases from spreading again.

entry.title: Beaver that bit ‘several people’ in New Jersey tests positive for rabies
entry.summary: 'On Sunday, May 3rd, it was reported that a beaver at Lake Henry had contact with several people and has since tested positive for rabies,' the Mahwah Health Department said.

entry.title: Even the unconscious brain can learn — and predict what you’ll say next
entry.summary: Neuronal recordings of people under anaesthesia show that their brains are processing words and sounds.

entry.title: Non-invasive profiling of the tumour microenvironment with spatial ecotypes
entry.summary: Multimodal machine learning reveals that tumour microenvironments can be decomposed into spatially organized multicellular ecosystems, termed spatial ecotypes, that can be accessed non-invasively via liquid biopsy and used to profile individual cancers a…

entry.title: Man Gets Horrific Case of Diarrhea After Overdosing on Experimental GLP-1
entry.summary: The man was going to the bathroom up to 30 times a day.

entry.title: 3 patients are being evacuated to Europe from cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: The U.N. health agency says three patients on a cruise ship with suspected hantavirus infections are being flown to the Netherlands. The ship involved in a deadly outbreak remains off Cape Verde. The World Health Organization says eight cases have been record…

entry.title: NYC measles case leads health officials to notify Manhattan restaurants, other venues
entry.summary: The outreach follows a positive case that officials have linked to foreign travel.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: A woman turned black and blue weeks after starting a new medication
entry.summary: A woman's sudden hyperpigmentation was a side effect of a medicine she was taking.

entry.title: Babies Are Bleeding to Death as Parents Reject a Vitamin Shot Given at Birth
entry.summary: A lifesaving injection given at birth to prevent severe bleeding has become collateral damage of the anti-vaccine movement.

entry.title: Colon cancer breakthrough keeps patients cancer-free for nearly 3 years
entry.summary: A short burst of immunotherapy before surgery is delivering surprisingly powerful results for a specific type of colorectal cancer. Patients in a UK-led trial who received just nine weeks of pembrolizumab prior to surgery have remained cancer-free nearly thre…

entry.title: Eggs and brain health: New study cracks open Alzheimer’s link
entry.summary: A long-term study suggests that eating eggs regularly may be associated with a lower risk of developing Alzheimer’s disease in older adults.

entry.title: Common knee surgery found ineffective, may make things worse
entry.summary: A major 10-year clinical trial is turning one of the world’s most common knee surgeries on its head. Researchers found that trimming a damaged meniscus—a procedure long believed to relieve pain—offers no real benefit over placebo surgery. Even more surprising…

entry.title: Spain agrees to let hantavirus-hit cruise ship dock in Canary Islands
entry.summary: Three people have died since the outbreak in April, while two crew members on board the ship require urgent care.

entry.title: HT Health Talk: Is there minimum and maximum age for Ozempic? Bengaluru doctor answers 10 FAQs about weight loss drugs | Health
entry.summary: HT Health Talk: GLP-1 drugs are revolutionary, but require nutritional monitoring and strength training to remain safe and effective, per a doctor. | Health

entry.title: FDA halts publication of studies on covid, shingles vaccines
entry.summary: News of the studies being pulled comes weeks after the CDC decided it wouldn’t publish a report showing last winter’s covid vaccine cut the likelihood of hospitalizations.

entry.title: Choice of Tea or Coffee Could Affect Risk of Osteoporosis in Older Women
entry.summary: Few older ladies would turn down a nice cuppa.

entry.title: These Hobbies Could Lower Your Alzheimer's Risk, New Study Suggests
entry.summary: Stimulating hobbies like exercise, socializing and reading may help lower Alzheimer's risk, according to a new study on midlife brain health.

entry.title: Sleep apnea may be quietly changing your body in an unexpected way, study finds
entry.summary: New research finds obstructive sleep apnea is associated with higher muscle mass index but lower muscle density, signaling possible metabolic dysfunction.

entry.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
entry.summary: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.

entry.title: 1 confirmed case of measles in Manhattan; person visited restaurant in Hell's Kitchen
entry.summary: A restaurant in Hell's Kitchen is warning diners of a possible exposure last month.

entry.title: An Existing Drug Could Be Repurposed to Help Treat Panic Attacks
entry.summary: When a panic attack hits, it can be a scary, overwhelming, debilitating experience – but an effective new treatment could be on the way, in the form of an antibiotic.

entry.title: Ozempic Is Killing Off Weight Loss Surgeries. That’s a Problem
entry.summary: The number of bariatric surgeries performed annually in the U.S. has taken a sharp drop lately, new research shows, coinciding with the rise of GLP-1 therapy.

entry.title: 5 things mosquito experts do every summer to avoid getting bitten
entry.summary: Looking for pest prevention strategies that work? Researchers share how they prevent mosquito bites and keep the bugs at bay on their properties.

entry.title: Study: Egg consumption is associated with a lower risk of Alzheimer’s Disease
entry.summary: Consumption of eggs is associated with a lower risk of being diagnosed with Alzheimer's Disease for those 65 years and older, according to researchers at Loma Linda University Health

entry.title: Doctor accidentally fixes patient’s irregular heartbeat — by sticking a finger in a very unexpected place
entry.summary: A 29-year-old man experiencing an irregular heartbeat had an unexpected solution in an area further below his heart.

entry.title: A Single Dose of Psilocybin Induces Lasting Brain Changes, New Study Suggests
entry.summary: Just one insightful psychedelic trip can have a profound impact on a person, and a new study goes some way to explaining why.

entry.title: One dose of psilocybin changes the human brain, leading to higher entropy
entry.summary: Researchers at UC San Francisco and Imperial College London have shown that a single dose of psilocybin, the psychedelic compound found in magic mushrooms, causes likely anatomical brain changes that last for up to a month after the experience.

entry.title: 'Age-old' disease hits 12-year high in Calif. as new outbreak details revealed
entry.summary: Tuberculosis cases have been increasing in California in recent years. The ancient bacterial disease can remain dormant for years.

entry.title: Ultrasensitive Test Detects Biomarkers for Specific Form of Dementia
entry.summary: Mass General Brigham researchers combine expertise in neurology, pathology to make strides for patients with frontotemporal lobar degeneration.

entry.title: The Science on Creatine Dosage Is Shifting. Here’s How Many Grams You Should Take.
entry.summary: Some wellness influencers recommend five times the usual. We talked to researchers for the real scoop.

entry.title: I’m a neuroscientist. Here’s how to maintain good cognitive health at any age
entry.summary: The secret to remaining mentally sharp could be as simple as cultivating habits that are beneficial to brain function

entry.title: I Have Terrible Allergies. These Products Are Helping Me Survive This Spring
entry.summary: My seasonal allergies have been exponentially easier to manage with these devices and products.

entry.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country
entry.summary: On 2 May 2026, a cluster of passengers with severe respiratory illness aboard a cruise ship was reported to the World Health Organization. The ship is carrying 147 passengers and crew. As of 4 May 2026, seven cases (two laboratory confirmed cases of hantaviru…

entry.title: Evidence mounts that Long Covid is damaging the hearts of those affected
entry.summary: There are also increasing signs that the condition can disrupt the autonomic nervous system

entry.title: 'Spreading like wildfire': Fiji grapples with soaring HIV cases
entry.summary: As evening falls in Fiji's capital, a steady stream of people approaches a makeshift clinic that is a first line of defence against one of the world's...

entry.title: What to know about hantavirus, the illness suspected in a cruise ship outbreak
entry.summary: Three people have died and others have been sickened after what some health authorities suspect may be a rare hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread mainly by contact with rodents. It…

entry.title: Cruise ship outbreaks hit 23 in 2025, with norovirus the most common virus
entry.summary: While taking a cruise is generally safe, having a high concentration of people in a semienclosed space makes it easy for viruses to spread among passengers.

entry.title: Cruise ship outbreaks hit 23 in 2025, with norovirus the most common virus
entry.summary: While taking a cruise is generally safe, having a high concentration of people in a semienclosed space makes it easy for viruses to spread among passengers.

entry.title: Cruise ship outbreaks hit 23 in 2025, with norovirus the most common virus
entry.summary: While taking a cruise is generally safe, having a high concentration of people in a semienclosed space makes it easy for viruses to spread among passengers.

entry.title: Inside Jordan Peterson’s ‘brutally painful’ illness as his wife reveals he’s suffering serious side effects from benzo withdrawal
entry.summary: “He misses the opportunity to weigh in on societal issues,” Tammy Peterson said of her husband.

entry.title: Patients remain cancer-free nearly 3 years after receiving experimental immunotherapy
entry.summary: Every participant in a new bowel cancer immunotherapy trial remained cancer-free nearly three years after receiving pembrolizumab before surgery.

entry.title: New tool can find deadliest cancer years before tumors can be seen on a scan
entry.summary: Many are split on whether AI is good for humanity, but a new AI-powered tool could prove revolutionary when it comes to cancer screenings.

entry.title: The dark side of the American work ethic: widespread sleep deprivation, linked to obesity, depression, even early death
entry.summary: From billions in lost productivity to rising healthcare costs, a widespread sleep deficit is undermining the American economy.

entry.title: Get ready to work for 60 years — as living to 100 becomes the new norm
entry.summary: America’s older population is expected to expand in the coming years. That will change how we work, save and live.

entry.title: Cranberry juice could slow the emergence of drug-resistant bacteria
entry.summary: The rise of bacteria is making it harder to combat conditions such as urinary tract infections

entry.title: Omega-3 supplements may be linked to faster cognitive decline in seniors, study finds
entry.summary: Omega-3 supplements are popular among many older adults to help combat age-related issues. They are often marketed as supporting cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia. However, a new study published in The Journal of Pr…

entry.title: Dads are dying after their kids are born, and no one is tracking it
entry.summary: New study examines paternal mortality data in Georgia, finds 60% of deaths were preventable

entry.title: This simple amino acid supplement greatly reduces Alzheimer’s damage
entry.summary: A new study suggests a surprisingly simple compound could help fight Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that arginine—an inexpensive amino acid already considered safe—can reduce the buildup of toxic amyloid proteins in the brain, a hallmark of the diseas…
